project: Financial Data ETL & Live Streaming Platform
author: Leonardo Vila
purpose: Professional Engineering Showcase — demonstrates ability to architect, optimize, and deploy complex asynchronous data infrastructure from scratch

data_ingestion.concurrency: asyncio event loop with persistent connection pool
data_ingestion.multiplexing: N symbols fired concurrently over single WS via unique chart/quote session IDs
data_ingestion.keepalive: Automatic ~h~N ping echo — failure to echo = TV closes connection with 1000 OK

storage.schema: 5 tables — tv_candles_raw, fundamentals_snapshot, performance_1d, volatility_1d, volume_1d
storage.write_pattern: Bulk executemany() upserts — one transaction per runner per cycle

compute.live_engine: Pure Pandas on a 258-row in-memory DataFrame (zero I/O)
compute.math_parity: Live compute imports LAGS, VOL_WINDOWS, SMA_WINDOWS, ANNUALIZATION_FACTOR directly from batch runners — single source of truth

api_layer.security: Origin whitelist + optional demo token (simple string comparison, not JWT — latency over ceremony)

frontend.state_management: Zustand 5 (transient updates — tick handler does NOT cause full store re-render)
frontend.charting: TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 (autoSize, O(1) series.update())
frontend.styling: Tailwind CSS v4 (CSS-first @theme config, zero tailwind.config.js)

infrastructure.ws_proxy: Nginx proxy_pass with Upgrade headers, proxy_read_timeout=3600s
infrastructure.deployment: Vite production build → scp to VPS, Uvicorn behind Nginx

phase_1_batch_etl.description: Nightly batch pipeline: scrapes 2,425 symbols from TradingView, persists OHLCV + fundamentals, computes 3 families of derived metrics

stages.name: Universe Resolution
stages.action: Load catalog.json → resolve 2,425 tickers with provider_symbol mappings

stages.name: Increment Plan
stages.action: Query SQLite for each symbol's last timestamp → determine n_candles needed (bootstrap=4500, catchup=3-25)

stages.name: WebSocket Scraping
stages.action: Pool of 6 persistent WS connections drains an asyncio.Queue of (symbol, n_candles) pairs. Each connection multiplexes OHLCV + fundamentals requests with 3-layer timeout protection.

stages.name: OHLCV Persistence
stages.action: Bulk upsert all candle rows via single executemany(). Calendar-aware partial bar detection using exchange_calendars (cached per exchange, max 3 instantiations).

stages.name: Fundamentals Persistence
stages.action: Extract market_cap, P/E, EPS, shares, sector, industry from TV quote snapshots → bulk upsert

stages.name: Derived Metrics (3 parallel runners)
stages.action: ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3) runs price_performance, volatility, and volume runners concurrently. Each runner: 1 bulk SQL read (ROW_NUMBER window function, PARTITION BY symbol, LIMIT per symbol) → 1 Pandas DataFrame → groupby().rolling()/shift() → 1 bulk executemany() write.
stages.query_reduction: 21,600 individual queries → 6 total (1 read + 1 write × 3 runners)

stages.name: Finalize
stages.action: Close DB, output execution report with per-stage timing

phase_2_live_streaming.description: Real-time WebSocket streaming: Seed & Edge architecture. Client connects → receives full historical chart (4,500 bars) + metrics from SQLite, then continuous live ticks from TradingView with sub-millisecond in-memory metric recomputation.

components.name: Seed (Cold Start)
components.action: 5 indexed SQL queries on WAL-mode SQLite → full chart + fundamentals + pre-computed metrics

components.name: Edge (Live Stream)
components.action: Async generator: subscribes to TradingView, yields ('seed'|'tick'|'fundamentals'|'company_name'|'heartbeat', data). Persistent connection with TV keepalive ping echo. Session-ID filtering rejects stale messages from old chart sessions.

events.tick: Live 'du' (data update) → incremental bar update, every 1-3 seconds during market hours
events.fundamentals: quote_completed → market_cap, P/E, EPS, shares, sector, industry
events.heartbeat: No data for 30s or ping-only message → keepalive signal

components.name: In-Memory State (Chalkboard)
components.action: LiveSymbolState holds a 258-row Pandas DataFrame in RAM. update_tick() replaces or appends the latest bar via O(1) .loc[] indexing. get_df_snapshot() returns a thread-safe copy for executor-based compute.

components.name: Live Compute (Calculator)
components.action: compute_all_metrics_live(df) → {performance, volatility, volume}. Pure functions, zero I/O. Imports constants from batch runners — mathematical parity guaranteed.
components.compute_latency: <1ms for all 3 metric families on 258 rows

components.name: Session Manager (Gatekeeper)
components.action: Each subscribe() call opens a dedicated TradingView WebSocket. No shared socket state — eliminates concurrent recv() crashes. Max 5 simultaneous streams (PoW demo limit).

components.name: Orchestrator (Crown Jewel)
components.action: FastAPI WebSocket handler. Seed phase: run_in_executor(load_historical_seed) → send_json. Edge phase: asyncio.wait races stream.__anext__() vs switch_event.wait() — symbol switch is INSTANT regardless of stream state. Zombie protection: 2h hard TTL, 5min idle warning, 10min idle disconnect.

phase_3_frontend_rendering.description: React SPA consumes the WebSocket. Zustand store distributes typed payloads to isolated component slices. Chart updates via O(1) series.update(). Mobile-first responsive grid.

components.name: WebSocket Store (Nervous System)
responsibilities: symbol switch via WS command (no reconnection)
message routing via discriminated union switch/case

components.name: Candlestick Chart
components.pattern: Created ONCE on mount, never destroyed. Seed → setData() (full replace with dedup+sort sanitization). Tick → update() (O(1)). autoSize: true for all viewport changes.

components.name: TickStack (Live Feed)
components.desktop: Persistent right sidebar, 50 rows, 4 columns, GPU-accelerated slideIn animation
components.mobile: Collapsible bottom-sheet via floating pill toggle, 20 rows, 3 columns, 44px touch targets

components.name: MetricsGrid
components.mobile: Scroll-snap tabs with IntersectionObserver sync — one card at a time, native iOS momentum

components.name: SymbolSearch
components.pattern: Persistent inline header bar — idle shows TICKER + company name + live price; click/tap transforms to search input with autocomplete dropdown. Case-insensitive filter on ticker AND company name. 16px font on mobile prevents iOS zoom.

performance_optimizations.title: Connection Pooling + Batch Multiplexing
performance_optimizations.after: 6 persistent connections, each processing hundreds of symbols via unique session IDs. All 6 sends (3 chart + 3 quote) fired upfront before any recv. Single unified receive loop routes by session ID in p[0].

performance_optimizations.title: N+1 Query Elimination via Mega-Batch SQL
performance_optimizations.before: 3 runners × 2,400 symbols × 3 queries each = 21,600 individual SQLite calls
performance_optimizations.after: 3 runners × 2 queries each = 6 total. ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY symbol ORDER BY ts DESC) limits rows per symbol INSIDE the SQL, not in Python.

performance_optimizations.title: Pandas Vectorized GroupBy (C-level operations)
performance_optimizations.before: Manual Python loops with math.sqrt, sum, and None-checking: O(n × sum(windows)) = ~750M Python comparisons
performance_optimizations.after: df.groupby('symbol')['close'].shift(lag) for returns. grouped.rolling(window=w, min_periods=w).std() for volatility. Single-pass vectorized operations in NumPy/Pandas C backend.

performance_optimizations.title: Three-Layer Timeout Shield
performance_optimizations.before: Bare while True: await ws.recv() with NO timeout. One run hung for 12 hours (43,701 seconds).
performance_optimizations.after: RECV_TIMEOUT=30s per message, SEND_TIMEOUT=10s per send, SYMBOL_TIMEOUT=60s outer wrapper. TimeoutError propagates to pool worker → reconnect → next symbol.

performance_optimizations.title: Parallel Derived Metric Runners
performance_optimizations.before: 3 metric runners executed sequentially: total = sum(times)

performance_optimizations.title: asyncio.wait Symbol Switch Racing
performance_optimizations.before: async for loop blocked in ws.recv() for up to 45s during heartbeats. Symbol switch delayed until next recv return.
performance_optimizations.after: asyncio.wait({next_task, switch_task}, return_when=FIRST_COMPLETED). Switch event wins instantly — pending recv task cancelled. Zero-delay symbol switching.

performance_optimizations.title: In-Memory Compute Pipeline (Zero Disk I/O)
performance_optimizations.description: After the initial SQLite seed (5-15ms), the live pipeline NEVER touches disk. 258-row DataFrame maintained in RAM. update_tick() → compute_all_metrics_live() → send_json() runs entirely in memory. Compute offloaded to ThreadPoolExecutor via run_in_executor() to keep the asyncio event loop free.

performance_optimizations.title: Zustand Transient Updates + Lightweight Charts O(1)
performance_optimizations.description: Components subscribe to individual Zustand slices (e.g., useWsStore(s => s.latestTick)). Tick updates only re-render TickStack and Chart — not the entire app. Lightweight Charts series.update() is O(1) — replaces or appends the last bar without reprocessing the entire dataset.

vite_production_build.env_strategy: .env.development (localhost, dev only) vs .env.production (domain URLs, build only). .env.local NEVER used — it leaks localhost into production bundles.

backend_security.cors: Conditional: ['*'] in DEBUG mode, whitelist in production
backend_security.ws_demo_token: Optional LIVE_DEMO_TOKEN env var — simple string comparison
backend_security.sqlite_pragmas: WAL mode + busy_timeout=5000ms on every API connection

technical_justifications.why_sqlite_not_postgres: The database lives on the same VPS as the API. SQLite in WAL mode supports concurrent readers during ETL writes. Zero network latency, zero connection pooling complexity, zero process management. For a single-VPS deployment serving <10 concurrent users, PostgreSQL adds operational overhead with zero benefit. The 5-15ms seed query latency proves the point.
technical_justifications.why_asyncio_not_celery: The ETL pipeline is a single-process, I/O-bound workload. asyncio with persistent WebSocket connections and an asyncio.Queue is the natural fit. Celery would add Redis/RabbitMQ as dependencies, require worker process management, and introduce serialization overhead for passing DataFrames between processes. The ThreadPoolExecutor for Pandas compute bridges the async/sync boundary cleanly.
technical_justifications.why_zustand_not_redux: Redux's boilerplate (action types, reducers, dispatch) is overkill for a real-time streaming UI. Zustand's create() with direct set() calls is 10x less code. The transient update pattern (components subscribe to individual slices) prevents full-tree re-renders on every tick — critical when ticks arrive every 1-3 seconds.
technical_justifications.why_dedicated_ws_not_shared: The original shared-socket architecture caused 'cannot call recv while another coroutine is already waiting' crashes on symbol switches and page reloads. The websockets library enforces single-consumer recv(). Dedicated connections per subscriber eliminate all concurrency bugs at the cost of ~1 additional TradingView connection per active user. With MAX_CONNECTIONS=5, this is 5 TV sockets — trivial.
technical_justifications.why_in_memory_not_disk_for_live: Writing every tick to SQLite and re-reading for metrics would add 10-50ms of disk I/O per tick cycle. The 258-row DataFrame fits in ~12KB of RAM. compute_all_metrics_live() executes in <1ms. The entire tick-to-client pipeline completes in <10ms. Disk I/O would be the dominant bottleneck by 10x.
technical_justifications.why_manual_ws_not_socket_io: Socket.IO adds protocol overhead (polling fallback, room management, auto-reconnection) that we don't need. Our WebSocket protocol is simple: server sends typed JSON messages ({type: 'seed'|'tick'|...}), client sends commands ({action: 'switch'|'ping'}). The discriminated union pattern gives us exhaustive type checking in TypeScript. Raw websockets library on Python + browser WebSocket API = zero dependencies, full control.

highlight_resolutions.title: Multiplexed OHLCV + Fundamentals
highlight_resolutions.impact: Sequential → overlapped I/O, 0.15-0.25s saved per symbol

highlight_resolutions.title: Per-Session Trace Files
highlight_resolutions.impact: Global file contention → zero shared state

highlight_resolutions.title: API Query Optimization
highlight_resolutions.impact: TTL cache, subquery reversal, explicit columns
